behaviors.description: Review input is scoped to changed lines only, not full modified files.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: diff parser/scoper -> review-input builder. A unit test of one helper can miss wiring bugs where unchanged lines leak in. Higher levels were rejected: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess, and it does not depend on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire local modules with a synthetic PR diff containing changed and unchanged regions; assert the produced review payload includes only changed hunks/lines and excludes unchanged file content.

behaviors.description: Inline comments attach to correct diff positions on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at GitHub API/diff-view boundary (position/line/side semantics). In-process mocks can accept invalid mappings that GitHub rejects or renders incorrectly. Higher levels were rejected: behavior text is about placement mechanics, not model-output quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed diff anchors and verify via API/UI fields that each comment is attached to the intended file/hunk/line in the diff view.

behaviors.description: Agent identifies at least one real defect in a PR containing a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment (defect detection quality). Stubbed/model-mocked responses would always pass and hide true miss rates. Workflow rejected because behavior requires one model decision outcome, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run analysis with the real model on a seeded buggy PR diff and assert at least one returned finding correctly targets the known bug location/category.

behaviors.description: Each summary finding maps to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state consistency (inline-post results -> summary assembler). Unit may miss run-scoped ID propagation bugs; real external infra is not required to reproduce. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is ID linkage, not model-quality dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Execute posting pipeline with deterministic findings and mocked/stubbed post responses carrying comment IDs; assert summary payload references only IDs returned in that run and one-to-one mapping is preserved.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Drop can occur during orchestration/serialization between modules, not only inside one pure function. Local module wiring is sufficient to catch it; real OS/network/model output is not required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Feed multiple inline findings through the end-to-end local summary-construction path and assert cardinality/content parity between inline findings produced and summary entries posted.

behaviors.description: Sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real PR with fixed precomputed findings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real PR and full API sequence. Real HTTP/auth/rate-limit/resource-state issues are system-boundary failures hidden by mocks. Agentic rejected by concrete text: findings are 'fixed precomputed', so no dependence on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the automation against a real repository PR using a fixed findings fixture; verify diff fetch, inline comment creation, and summary comment creation all complete and are visible via GitHub API.

behaviors.description: Full live run with model-generated findings completes correctly for a real PR without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Execution path includes real model-generated findings, so mock substitution would hide model-dependent failures (empty/invalid/low-signal findings that break downstream logic). System alone is insufficient because correctness depends on actual AI output content. Workflow rejected by text: only one agentic decision stage (analyze) is specified; remaining steps are deterministic posting.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end on a real PR with the production model enabled; assert the run autonomously produces findings, posts inline comments, and posts a coherent summary linked to generated findings in one unattended execution.
